raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.3,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope should I prioritize: (A) light-touch sentence-level polish only (grammar, clarity, concision; primarily R1.6), or (B) comprehensive improvement including conceptual reframing, logic/evidence checks, and section-level reorganization in addition to polish (R1.1–R1.6)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The draft introduces a new evaluation construct, so refining the high-level thesis and contribution framing is central to improving it.",
    "R1.2": "Claims linking assumptions (e.g., independence) to conclusions and recommendations require internal logical consistency checks.",
    "R1.3": "Stated performance ranges and takeaways should align clearly with what is actually reported from the injection-recovery set.",
    "R1.5": "Section- and subsection-level ordering may need adjustment to strengthen the narrative arc from problem to method to implication.",
    "R1.6": "The request to improve the draft makes sentence-level clarity, concision, and readability a load-bearing responsibility.",
    "RX.1": "Edits should surface key assumptions and limits so uncertainty is transparent to readers.",
    "RX.2": "Wording must avoid overclaiming around novelty and recommendations to keep conclusions proportionate to evidence.",
    "RX.3": "All work must stay within the scope of improving the provided draft rather than expanding the study itself.",
    "RX.4": "Recommendations should be phrased to minimize risk of misuse or overreliance in operational decisions.",
    "RX.5": "Any changes should preserve traceability to the draft’s stated methods and results so readers can verify provenance."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope should I prioritize: (A) light-touch sentence-level polish only (grammar, clarity, concision; primarily R1.6), or (B) comprehensive improvement including conceptual reframing, logic/evidence checks, and section-level reorganization in addition to polish (R1.1–R1.6)?

rationale.R1.1: The draft introduces a new evaluation construct, so refining the high-level thesis and contribution framing is central to improving it.
rationale.R1.2: Claims linking assumptions (e.g., independence) to conclusions and recommendations require internal logical consistency checks.
rationale.R1.3: Stated performance ranges and takeaways should align clearly with what is actually reported from the injection-recovery set.
rationale.R1.5: Section- and subsection-level ordering may need adjustment to strengthen the narrative arc from problem to method to implication.
rationale.R1.6: The request to improve the draft makes sentence-level clarity, concision, and readability a load-bearing responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Edits should surface key assumptions and limits so uncertainty is transparent to readers.
rationale.RX.2: Wording must avoid overclaiming around novelty and recommendations to keep conclusions proportionate to evidence.
rationale.RX.3: All work must stay within the scope of improving the provided draft rather than expanding the study itself.
rationale.RX.4: Recommendations should be phrased to minimize risk of misuse or overreliance in operational decisions.
rationale.RX.5: Any changes should preserve traceability to the draft’s stated methods and results so readers can verify provenance.
